what_it_does: States the repo's conceptual foundation and living principles: what the system is, what it believes, and how every change should preserve those beliefs.
observations: Frontmatter marks it as a prescriptive, living concept note with aliases and related links.
Defines the repo as one entity with one brain and multiple faces: runtime, MCP agent face, and knowledge/Obsidian vault face.
Claims the system is built to be run and grown entirely by AI, with no human developers.
Describes the system as self-hosting and recursive: it reads and grows its own codebase.
Modules relate through seams, and the knowledge graph makes those relations traversable.
Lists nine embodied principles, each with a parenthetical pointer to where it is active in the repo.
Explicitly open to extension: new principles should be added when they emerge.
standouts: One entity, many faces, one brain framing.
Built to be run and grown entirely by AI.
Governed self-modification: agent proposes, operator disposes.
Prove by use: acceptance suites as convergence record, fresh evidence required.
conventions: Uses Obsidian/wiki-link syntax for relational references.
Frontmatter carries type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, and status.
Practices relational description: link the single source rather than duplicate lists.
Declares itself a living document, meant to be extended rather than frozen.
concerns: Some parenthetical pointers are vague, e.g., root rules, corpus/twin work, RHM.
References an external design vault context without including it, so readers must locate it.
Does not itself version its principles, despite preaching schema-additive, non-breaking change.
notes: This is the repo's philosophical constitution: it defines what the system believes and how every change should keep those beliefs true.
questions: What are the exact five invariants in build-prep context-06-the-foundation-laws?
What is the specific mechanism by which the Coherence Gate enforces semantic truth?
Who or what are Tim, the crew, and the RHM in this context?
What are the exact root rules referenced in principles 1, 3, and 4?
What does the acceptance-suite evidence look like for prove-by-use?
intention_signals: Establish a durable value framework so autonomous agents can make consistent, governed decisions while extending the system without duplicating or forking it.
novelty: Treats the documentation vault itself as a face of the same brain rather than a separate docs system, and asserts the repo is built to be run and grown entirely by AI.
contribution: Provides the prescriptive why layer that anchors decisions across the repo, linking concept to rules, map, conventions, and coherence enforcement so principles are navigable and actionable.
summary_for_embedding: company/docs/concepts-and-principles.md is a living prescriptive concept note that defines the repo as a reactive, self-hosting, AI-operated composition substrate with one brain and multiple faces, and enumerates nine governing principles for changes.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to give agents and operators a shared conceptual foundation and decision framework for extending the system without duplicating or breaking it.
